commit.message:
docs(ux): retire village and editor docs

Summary:
- rewrite the source-of-truth docs so Seraph is cockpit-only and the village/editor line is explicitly retired
- archive or demote legacy village-first pages in the legacy docs tree and sidebar instead of keeping them mixed with current guidance
- replace removed village/editor guides with short archive stubs and update README/editor docs to match the current product contract

Rationale:
- the repo, roadmap, and generated docs should stop implying there are two live UI directions when the product is now cockpit-only
- archive material is still useful as historical context, but it needs a clear boundary so contributors do not treat it as current guidance

Tests:
- git diff --check
- cd docs && npm run build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: seraph
repository.description: AI agent with a retro 16-bit RPG village UI — Phaser 3 canvas, persistent identity, long-term memory, hierarchical goals, and plug-and-play MCP server integration

commit.message:
fix(operator): harden timeline session continuity

Summary:
- normalize operator timeline timestamps so queued native notifications work with their real stored ISO string values
- scope recent intervention loading by session before limiting so session-specific timelines keep linked continuity items
- add endpoint and repository regressions for string notification timestamps, session-scoped intervention loading, and queued insight thread mapping

Rationale:
- the new operator timeline would 500 whenever pending native notifications existed because their created_at field is stored as a string
- session-filtering after a global intervention cap could silently drop relevant intervention-linked items from thread-specific timelines

Tests:
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest tests/test_operator_api.py tests/test_guardian_feedback.py
- cd backend && OPENROUTER_API_KEY=test-key WORKSPACE_DIR=/tmp/seraph-test UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest -v

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(platform): ship operator timeline and preflight batch

Summary:
- add threaded operator timeline, workflow replay metadata, capability preflight, and autorepair surfaces across the backend and cockpit
- extend provider safeguards plus guardian world-model and learning-policy signals, and refresh the implementation roadmap for the next batch

Rationale:
- make Seraphs growing workflow and capability surface more operable and legible without weakening execution guardrails
- keep the docs and eval harness aligned with the shipped operator, runtime, and guardian behavior

Tests:
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest tests/test_capabilities_api.py tests/test_workflows.py tests/test_operator_api.py tests/test_guardian_state.py tests/test_llm_runtime.py
- cd frontend && npm test -- src/components/cockpit/CockpitView.test.tsx src/stores/panelLayoutStore.test.ts src/components/cockpit/layouts.test.ts
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest tests/test_eval_harness.py
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run python -m src.evals.harness --scenario workflow_approval_threading_behavior --scenario threaded_operator_timeline_behavior --scenario capability_preflight_behavior --scenario provider_policy_safeguards --indent 2
- cd backend && OPENROUTER_API_KEY=test-key WORKSPACE_DIR=/tmp/seraph-test UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest -v

commit.message:
docs(changelog): record recovery fix hash

Summary:
- replace the remaining changelog placeholder with the short hash for
- the recovery and redundant-bounce fix commit

Rationale:
- the repo changelog rules require release-note-worthy entries to point
- at the concrete change hash before handoff

Validation:
- git rev-parse --short HEAD
- git status --short

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(recovery): heal held slaves and split redundant bounces

Summary:
- keep slave health polling active while slaves are held in PREOP
- or SAFEOP and align simulator docs/tests with PREOP-first
- reconnect healing
- keep a bounded merge window for complementary redundant logical
- bounce replies so split-ring raw topologies can recover full WKC
- suppress duplicate raw simulator exclusion warnings in test helper

Rationale:
- held slave states still need to surface disconnects and lower AL-state
- regressions instead of leaving stale runtime state behind
- split redundant bounce replies can each carry partial logical payload, so
- accepting the first processed bounce too early causes false recovery
- duplicate interface warnings added noise without improving test feedback

Validation:
- mix test --failed
- mix test

commit.message:
fix(frontend): wait for macro action button

Summary:
- make the operator-terminal macro test wait for the rendered button role instead of raw text timing

Rationale:
- CI was failing on a brittle text lookup in the cockpit test even though the control exists in the rendered operator surface
- button-role queries are a closer match to the actual UI semantics and more stable on slower runners

Tests:
- cd frontend && npm test
- cd frontend && npm test -- src/components/cockpit/CockpitView.test.tsx

fix(frontend): deflake workflow approval continuity test

Summary:
- make the workflow approval continuity test wait on the actual async session-switch and draft-queue boundaries
- keep the CI frontend suite aligned with the slower GitHub runner timing

Rationale:
- frontend-tests was failing in CI while the underlying product path was green locally
- the new cockpit test relied on the default 1s wait around a thread switch and draft enqueue path, which is too tight on the Actions runner

Tests:
- cd frontend && npm test
- for i in 1 2 3; do (cd frontend && npm test -- src/components/cockpit/CockpitView.test.tsx) || exit 1; done

commit.message:
feat(slave): own reconnect healing and held-state monitoring

Summary:
- move reconnect ownership into the slave runtime using stored
  position probes, anonymous fixed-station reclaim, and local PREOP
  rebuilds
- remove master reconnect authorization and rediscovery fallback from
  the active reconnect path, and align docs and tests to that model
- keep PREOP and SAFEOP health polling active so disconnects and
  lower-state regressions enter recovery instead of stranding stale
  held states

Rationale:
- the common power-loss return case is local to one slave and is
  simpler to heal in one place than to orchestrate through the master
- intentionally held PREOP and SAFEOP sessions are still live runtime
  states, so they should keep fault visibility instead of going silent

Validation:
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors
- mix test test/ethercat/slave_test.exs test/ethercat/master_test.exs
  test/ethercat/master_recovery_bus_test.exs
  test/integration/simulator/05_safeop_retreat_with_health_poll_test.exs
  test/integration/simulator/03_disconnect_with_health_poll_test.exs
  test/integration/simulator/43_permanent_slave_disconnect_recovery_stuck_test.exs

commit.message:
fix(bus): fast-path degraded redundant realtime bounces

Summary:
- let redundant realtime exchanges complete immediately on a single
  processed bounce instead of waiting for timeout-driven best effort
- add unit coverage for the realtime one-bounce fast path
- extend the secondary-disconnect simulator regression to a 1ms domain

Rationale:
- after the earlier timeout-budget fixes, degraded redundant operation
  was still too conservative for 1ms cyclic traffic
- cyclic PDO exchanges should favor low-latency processed data, while
  reliable/startup traffic can keep the stricter merge behavior
- this keeps the fix local to the bus link and avoids adding NIC-state
  probing just to distinguish backup-port disconnect from other cases

Validation:
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors
- mix test test/ethercat/bus_test.exs
- mix test test/integration/simulator/45_redundant_secondary_disconnect_cycle_budget_test.exs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
docs(changelog): fill degraded redundant hardening hash

Summary:
- replace the pending unreleased changelog hashes with the new runtime
  hardening commit id

Rationale:
- keep the unreleased notes traceable to the feature commit that added
  the degraded redundant timing and receive-error fixes

Validation:
- not run (changelog-only follow-up)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(bus): harden degraded redundant raw handling

Summary:
- surface raw receive errors through the bus transport boundary and
  degrade single/redundant links explicitly instead of silently
  ignoring receive-side failures
- keep degraded redundant exchanges within the original frame timeout
  budget and pass that bus timeout budget down to cyclic domains
- add missing secondary-break simulator coverage, redundant raw helper
  toggles, and a 10ms secondary-disconnect regression scenario

Rationale:
- a secondary-port disconnect on real hardware could keep a 10ms
  redundant ring timing out even though the primary path was still
  usable
- the root cause was split across layers: redundant links extended the
  merge wait past the exchange budget, and domains could time out
  their Bus.transaction call before the bus budget had expired
- explicit receive-error handling and transport-aware simulator
  coverage make the degraded path easier to reason about and harder to
  regress again

Validation:
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors
- mix test test/ethercat/bus_test.exs test/ethercat/simulator_redundancy_test.exs
- mix test test/ethercat/domain_test.exs test/ethercat/master/startup_test.exs test/ethercat/master_test.exs
- mix test test/integration/simulator/45_redundant_secondary_disconnect_cycle_budget_test.exs
- mix test --failed

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
docs(changelog): fill runtime hardening hash

Summary:
- replace the three unreleased pending-hash placeholders with cf4a90c

Rationale:
- keep the changelog consistent with the committed runtime change set

Validation:
- not run (changelog-only follow-up)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(runtime): harden redundant links and recovery timing

Summary:
- delay master recovery until a domain crosses a short unhealthy streak,
  and keep a wider 5ms startup/activation frame-timeout floor
- make redundant links raw-only, require authoritative forward-path data,
  and drain both legs before dispatching the next exchange
- enable PACKET_IGNORE_OUTGOING on raw sockets and align simulator
  scenarios/docs with passthrough semantics and raw transport controls

Rationale:
- avoid simulator and raw transport flapping on isolated cycle misses and
  transient SAFEOP/startup timeouts
- keep healthy-ring redundant semantics explicit while cutting stale
  previous-exchange traffic that was inflating idx_mismatch drops

Validation:
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors
- mix docs --warnings-as-errors --formatter html
- mix test test/ethercat/bus_test.exs
- mix test test/ethercat/bus/link/redundant_merge_test.exs test/ethercat/simulator_redundancy_test.exs
- mix test test/ethercat/domain/health_notifications_test.exs test/ethercat/master_test.exs test/ethercat/master/startup_test.exs test/ethercat/master_recovery_bus_test.exs test/ethercat/master_activation_test.exs test/ethercat/master/config_test.exs
- mix test test/ethercat/domain_test.exs
- mix test test/integration/simulator/38_redundant_passthrough_only_partial_test.exs test/integration/simulator/41_redundant_multi_datagram_bwr_test.exs

commit.message:
Reapply mOTLP for ECH GA changes (#5487)

## Summary
- Reapplies the mOTLP for ECH GA docs updates after the accidental
direct merge/revert on `main`.
- Restores the intended content updates across quickstart,
troubleshooting, and solution index/snippet pages.
- Supersedes the accidentally merged-and-reverted path from #4946 with a
reviewable PR flow.

## Test plan
- [x] Confirm `git diff origin/main...HEAD` contains only the intended 4
docs files.
- [x] Verify branch is pushed and opens cleanly against `main`.
- [ ] Run CI checks in GitHub and confirm all required checks pass.
- [ ] Validate docs preview links for the changed pages.

## Context
- Previous PR: #4946

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

commit.message:
fix(mps): eliminate MPS CPU fallbacks for `grid_sample` and bicubic backward (#821)

- `grid_sampler_2d_backward`: backward pass of `F.grid_sample` not yet
  implemented on MPS.  Replaced with `_bilinear_grid_sample` in
  `utilities/tensors.py` — gather-based index arithmetic that is native
  on every backend.  Non-MPS devices delegate to `F.grid_sample` unchanged
  (CUDA fused kernel preserved).  Affects deformable attention fallback
  (`ms_deform_attn_core_pytorch`) and segmentation point sampling
  (`point_sample`).
- `_upsample_bicubic2d_aa_backward`: anti-aliased bicubic backward not
  implemented on MPS.  Conditionally disable `antialias` on MPS in both
  DINOv2 position embedding interpolation paths (`dinov2.py`,
  `dinov2_with_windowed_attn.py`).

Adds 35 parity tests in `tests/utilities/test_tensors.py` verifying the
manual bilinear path matches `F.grid_sample` numerically (atol=1e-5) and
passes `torch.autograd.gradcheck`.

- Move padding_mode validation before shape unpack for fail-fast behaviour
- Cast bilinear weights to input.dtype to preserve float16 on MPS
- Fix mislabelled bicubic comment in dinov2.py
- Update stale point_sample docstring to reference _bilinear_grid_sample

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: pre-commit-ci[bot] <66853113+pre-commit-ci[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: align architecture docs and simplify changelog

Summary:
- align ARCHITECTURE, README, RELEASE, AGENTS, and simulator docs to
  the current public/runtime boundaries and release flow
- simplify the Unreleased changelog section so entries are grouped by
  user-visible outcomes instead of commit-first bullets

Rationale:
- the docs had drifted from the current FSM split, simulator transport
  ownership, and Hex publish workflow
- the changelog was getting harder to scan because commit hashes were
  leading each entry instead of supporting the release notes

Validation:
- mix docs --warnings-as-errors --formatter html

feat(stt): requeue audio on socket closure

Harden the AssemblyAI streaming path so audio chunks are not lost when the socket closes during a send. Requeue failed chunks at the front of the buffer, reconnect automatically, and add regression coverage for preserving chunk order after a failed send.

commit.message:
feat(sip): hang up calls explicitly on local end

Send SIP BYE as part of the local call termination path instead of relying only on final cleanup. Add a single-flight hangup helper so browser-triggered end, successful reservation end, and fallback teardown do not send duplicate BYE requests, and cover the helper with tests.

commit.message:
replace TargSubsRaw with DialogueView to fix STT/translit regression

Commit 206e3bd introduced TargSubsRaw to preserve non-Default ASS styles during
transliteration, but this created a deep-copy snapshot that never received STT
updates from processDubtitles. When dubtitles and transliteration were both
enabled, romanized output was based on the original subtitle text rather than
the STT/ASR transcript the user had requested.

The root cause was maintaining two diverging copies of the subtitle structure.
This replaces the two-copy model with a single canonical TargSubs (never
filtered in-place) plus a DialogueView that provides indexed access to Default-
style dialogue items only. Since view items are pointers into the canonical
structure, STT mutations through the view propagate automatically to all
consumers, including transliteration.

∙ add DialogueView type to subs package with NewDialogueView, Item, Len,
  RawIndex methods
∙ add IsDefaultStyle as exported function in subs package
∙ add Subs2DubsFromView that operates through the view
∙ remove TargSubsRaw from Task struct
∙ prepareSubtitles creates DialogueView instead of deep-copy + in-place filter
∙ Supervisor, ProcessItem, and all item counting go through DialogueView
∙ GetCompleteSubtitleText and CreateSubtitlesFromText take DialogueView for
  index mapping
∙ summary and condensed-audio paths use dialogue-only items

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
